zh.lifeSummary: Lady Katherine Chamberlain於大約1505年出生在威爾士拉德諾斯爾。她於大約1524年與Thomas Anton Peake II在英國威爾斯拉德諾斯爾普雷斯泰尼結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和12個女兒的父母。她去世於1590年在威爾士拉德諾斯爾普雷斯泰尼享年86歲，埋葬在英國坎布利其爾巴辛波。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Katherine Chamberlain于大约1505年出生在威尔士拉德諾斯爾。她于大约1524年与Thomas Anton Peake II在英国威尔斯拉德諾斯爾普雷斯泰尼结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和12个女儿的父母。她去世于1590年在威尔士拉德諾斯爾普雷斯泰尼享年86岁，埋葬在英国坎布利其爾巴辛波。

nameMeanings.name: Chamberlain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name from Old French chambrelain, Norman French cambrelanc, cambrelen(c) ‘chamberlain’ (of ancient Germanic origin, from kamer ‘chamber, room’, Latin camera (see Chambers ) + the diminutive suffix -(l)ing). This was originally the name of an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, but is so widespread in late medieval England that it must sometimes have been used of people of more ordinary status, perhaps as a nickname for an officious or self-important person or for someone who played the role of chamberlain in a folk play, tableau, or ceremony. Compare Chancellor for a possible similar usage.

variants.name: Chamberlin
meanings: French: occupational name for an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, from Old French chamberlenc ‘chamberlain’. See also Chamberlain and compare above.

variants.name: Chambers
meanings: from Middle English chaumbre ‘room (in a house); reception room in a palace or official building’ (Old French chambre). It is identical in implied function with the Chamberlain , which denoted an official: to pay in cameram was to pay into the exchequer, of which the camerarius or chamberer was in charge. The surname also applied to clerks employed there. As the office of Chamberlain rose in the social scale, this term remained reserved for more humble servants of the bedchamber or private quarters.
(of Norman origin): habitational name from Les Chambres, Manche (France).

nameMeanings.name: Katherine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of   Katherine  , also occasionally used in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Lady Katherine Chamberlain was born about 1505, in Radnorshire, Wales. She married Thomas Anton Peake II about 1524, in Presteigne, Radnorshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 12 daughters. She died in 1590, in Presteigne, Radnorshire, Wales, at the age of 86, and was buried in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Katherine Chamberlain est née environ 1505, à Radnorshire, Galles. Elle a épousé Thomas Anton Peake II environ 1524, à Presteigne, Radnorshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 12 filles. Elle est décédée en 1590, à Presteigne, Radnorshire, Galles, à l’âge de 86 ans, et a été enterrée à Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Katherine Chamberlainは約1505年にウェールズラドノーシュアイアで生まれた。約1524年にイギリスウェールズラドノーシュアイアプレステイグンでThomas Anton Peake IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と12人の娘の親であった。彼女は1590年にウェールズラドノーシュアイアプレステイグンで86歳で死去し，イギリスカンブリジェシュアイアバシングボウルンにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Ann and Saint Agnes with Saint Joh: 영국 런던 세인트 아 앤드 세인트 애그니스 위스 세인트 존 재커리
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Katherine Chamberlain(은)는 대략 1505년 웨일스 라드노르실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1524년 영국 웨일스 라드노르실르 프레스테익니에서 Thomas Anton Peake II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1590년 웨일스 라드노르실르 프레스테익니에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 캄브리지실르 배징보우른에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Katherine Chamberlain nasceu aproximadamente 1505, em Radnorshire, Gales. Ela casou-se com Thomas Anton Peake II aproximadamente 1524, em Presteigne, Radnorshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 12 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1590, em Presteigne, Radnorshire, Gales, com 86 anos, e foi sepultada em Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Saint Ann and Saint Agnes with Saint Joh: сеинт анн анд сеинт агнесь вит сеинт джон зачэри, Лондон, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Lady Katherine Chamberlain родилась около 1505 г. в местности радноршир, Уэльс. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Anton Peake II около 1524 г. в местности престиин, радноршир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 12 дочерей. Она умерла в 1590 г. в местности престиин, радноршир, Уэльс в возрасте 86 лет и была похоронена в местности бассингбурн, камбриджешир, Англия.

Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 12 hijas. Murió en 1590, en Presteigne, Radnorshire, Gales, a la edad de 86 años, y fue sepultada en Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra.
